llcFilesWith: Oklahoma Secretary of State (Trade Name Report, SOS Form 0021)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Oklahoma Secretary of State as well; individuals and partnerships file the same Trade Name Report centrally, and Oklahoma has no county-level DBA filing
feeBasis: The $25 Secretary of State fee registers a trade name for an LLC, corporation, or other filer. The registration has no expiration and is not renewed.
renewalRule: A Trade Name Report does not expire and is not renewed; it remains on file until the filer withdraws it while the underlying entity stays in good standing.
protectionNote: A Trade Name Report is a notice filing; exclusive rights in a name come from a trademark or service mark registered under Title 78, not from the trade name report. It does carry a practical effect: because § 2008 screens new entity names against trade, fictitious, and reserved names, a registered trade name blocks a later entity or reserved name that is not distinguishable from it while active.
searchNote: Trade names registered with the Secretary of State appear in the same business records search as entities, since they are part of the § 2008 distinguishability records. Oklahoma keeps DBA filings entirely at the state level, so there is no separate county index to check.
notes: Oklahoma runs a clean state-level DBA system: every filer, whether an LLC, corporation, sole proprietor, or partnership, registers a Trade Name Report with the Secretary of State for $25 (Oklahoma calls an entity's DBA a "trade name" and an individual's or partnership's a "fictitious name," but both go on the same central report). The registration never expires and there is no newspaper-publication requirement. Because trade and fictitious names sit in the same records the state screens for distinguishability, a registered trade name behaves defensively like an entity name while active, though it grants no trademark right of its own.

sources.title: Oklahoma Statutes — Trade names (entity doing business under a name other than its legal name)
